document_meta.title: XtraType Centralized Inventory
document_meta.source_basis: Derived from the authoritative XtraType expanded frontend specification pack and aligned to the original XtraType description and prior foundational inventory.
document_meta.design_scope: Authoritative frontend product grammar, surfaces, view systems, templates, domain packs, automation UX, governance UX, public presentation modes, and the full sanctioned expansion universe.

maturity_scale.core: Foundational concept required across the mature frontend system.
maturity_scale.recommended: Broadly expected concept in normal XtraType deployments and surfaces.
maturity_scale.advanced: Expanded concept that is fully sanctioned in the product universe and may appear in more specialized packs or surfaces.
maturity_scale.optional: Useful in select interfaces or deployments but not required in every surface.

ui_visibility_scale.always_available: Should usually be present in the visible creation or reading interface.
ui_visibility_scale.context_driven: Should appear when the current target, intent, pack, workflow, or surface warrants it.
ui_visibility_scale.admin_or_system: Usually managed through configuration, stewardship, or deeper management surfaces rather than casual creation flows.
ui_visibility_scale.derived_or_internal: Captured or inferred by the system and primarily revealed through inspectors, badges, or detail views.

field_guide.id: Stable unique identifier for the inventory item.
field_guide.section: Major grouping used in the human-readable inventory.
field_guide.kind: Normalized item class within the inventory model.
field_guide.name: Canonical display name.
field_guide.parent_id: Immediate parent concept when the item participates in a hierarchy.
field_guide.maturity: Implementation maturity and breadth within the frontend universe.
field_guide.ui_visibility_hint: How often the item should surface in user-facing interfaces.
field_guide.purpose: Why the concept belongs in the product and what it unlocks.
field_guide.depends_on: Related concepts that should exist or be understood first.
field_guide.implementation_notes: Short specification-oriented notes for downstream component and flow design.

next_spec_layers_suggested: Frontend component contracts and block-level schemas
Public presentation and overlay density rules
Prototype test scripts for trust, provenance, and review flows
API request and optimistic-state behavior contracts

items.name: Target
items.summary: Anchor object that an entry refers to, overlays, or is attached to.
items.purpose: Keeps the subject of reference distinct from authored content so one target can support many records, views, and public or private interpretations.
implementation_notes: Target identity must remain stable across capture, editing, overlay, and publication surfaces.
Composite targets are first-class targets, not temporary frontend hacks.

items.name: Entry
items.summary: User-created unit of content attached to a target or another entry.
items.purpose: Provides the universal authored record container for notes, evidence, citations, observations, workflow actions, media, and publishable contextual posts.
implementation_notes: Every surface must be able to produce a valid draft entry.
Entries may begin lightweight and deepen over time without losing identity.

items.name: Intent
items.summary: Declared, suggested, or inferred purpose of an entry.
items.purpose: Drives field emphasis, validation cues, drawer behavior, template defaults, review semantics, and downstream interpretation.
implementation_notes: Intent must stay editable in one click.
The same entry class may support multiple intents.

items.name: Context
items.summary: Situational frame that explains where, how, and under what conditions an entry was created and is being used.
items.purpose: Allows the frontend to adapt capture, view logic, template choice, automation, privacy cues, and disclosure by surface and domain.
implementation_notes: Captured facts and inferred domain context should remain visually distinguishable.
Context changes the surface feel without changing the underlying grammar.

items.name: Governance
items.summary: Control layer for trust, privacy, workflow, moderation, publication, and auditability.
items.purpose: Keeps an ultra-flexible contextual record system coherent, reviewable, explainable, and safe in private, team, and public settings.
implementation_notes: Governance is additive to authoring and should not dominate lightweight capture.
Important state changes must be inspectable and reversible from the frontend.

items.name: Target Family
items.summary: Top-level family for all things that can be referenced, addressed, overlaid, or revisited.
items.purpose: Provides the common abstraction across digital, spatial, temporal, named, abstract, object-memory, and composite subjects.
implementation_notes: Families are independently extensible without changing the core grammar.

items.name: Entry Family
items.summary: Top-level family for all user-authored content units.
items.purpose: Unifies notes, claims, citations, evidence, classifications, workflow actions, packets, and publishable contextual posts.
implementation_notes: Entry family items share one adaptive composer shell.

items.name: Relationship Family
items.summary: Top-level family for explicit links between targets, entries, sources, collections, and states.
items.purpose: Supports support, contradiction, reply, supersession, attribution, containment, aliasing, and graph-aware navigation.
implementation_notes: Relationships are first-class product structures, not hidden metadata.

items.name: Source Family
items.summary: Top-level family for materials that can be cited, extracted, or treated as evidence.
items.purpose: Keeps source identity and evidence support distinct from commentary and target identity.
implementation_notes: Sources must remain inspectable and reusable across many entries.

items.name: Context Record Family
items.summary: Top-level family for captured situational metadata and environmental traces.
items.purpose: Preserves time, device, geospatial, session, route, overlay, and import facts without bloating entry bodies.
implementation_notes: Context records power trust and continuity across surfaces.

items.name: Identity and Authorship Family
items.summary: Top-level family for actors, attribution modes, and visible roles.
items.purpose: Handles named, pseudonymous, anonymous, organizational, public, reviewer, and system participation.
implementation_notes: Display identity and trust identity must remain conceptually distinct.

items.name: Workflow and Moderation Family
items.summary: Top-level family for operational states, routes, queues, and review actions.
items.purpose: Supports drafting, review, moderation, escalation, publication, assignment, and resolution lifecycles.
implementation_notes: Workflow state is a visible dimension of records, not an afterthought.

items.name: Permissions and Audience Family
items.summary: Top-level family for audience boundaries, access scopes, and action capabilities.
items.purpose: Lets the frontend explain who can see, edit, publish, review, export, or reuse a record or target.
implementation_notes: The frontend renders permissions even though enforcement lives behind the API boundary.

items.name: Taxonomy and Schema Family
items.summary: Top-level family for controlled vocabularies, templates, packs, and front-end field structures.
items.purpose: Enables growth without chaos by making type systems and template systems visible, editable, and governable.
implementation_notes: Controlled growth is a core frontend feature.

items.name: Provenance and Trust Family
items.summary: Top-level family for origin, integrity, confidence, verification, and dispute signals.
items.purpose: Supports explainability and trust decisions for everyday capture as well as public or evidentiary use.
implementation_notes: Captured, inferred, asserted, imported, and reviewed data must remain visually distinct.

items.name: Digital Resource Target
items.summary: Target addressed through a digital locator or application-level identifier.
items.purpose: Covers web pages, documents, messages, records, files, and API-addressable resources.
implementation_notes: Support canonicalization, aliasing, snapshots, and quote anchoring.

items.name: Spatial / Geographic Target
items.summary: Target anchored in physical space or geographic coordinates.
items.purpose: Enables field observations, routes, geofences, site records, and place-based public layers.
implementation_notes: Point, area, and path should behave as compatible spatial primitives.

items.name: Temporal Target
items.summary: Target defined by a point, span, offset, or recurring window in time.
items.purpose: Supports event moments, historical periods, meeting windows, media offsets, and route timing.
implementation_notes: Absolute and relative time should remain distinguishable.

items.name: Named Entity Target
items.summary: Target identified primarily by canonical identity, alias, or public name.
items.purpose: Handles people, organizations, projects, products, topics, classrooms, devices, and locations with name-based identity.
implementation_notes: Allow aliases, external IDs, and display-name variants.

items.name: Abstract / Logical Target
items.summary: Target that is conceptual rather than directly locational.
items.purpose: Lets users attach records to claims, issues, policies, requirements, decisions, and arguments.
implementation_notes: These targets usually require stronger relationship modeling than address-only targets.

items.name: Object-Memory Target
items.summary: Target representing a physical object or item that can be revisited over time.
items.purpose: Powers scan-based memory, maintenance history, field inspection, and place-object linkage.
implementation_notes: Should support scan anchors, serial references, and physical context overlays.

items.name: Composite Target
items.summary: Layered target composed from more than one addressing primitive or target class.
items.purpose: Supports the real-world cases where meaning is attached to a combination of page, quote, time, place, object, or event.
implementation_notes: Composite targets are a core part of the product, not an exceptional edge case.

items.name: Annotation Entry
items.summary: Local commentary attached to a target context.
items.purpose: Supports lightweight remarks, contextual notes, and inline interpretation without forcing rigorous structure.
implementation_notes: Optimized for fast capture and later deepening.

items.name: Citation Entry
items.summary: Entry whose main role is source-aware attribution or reference.
items.purpose: Makes evidence and supporting sources explicit and reusable.
implementation_notes: Needs bibliographic, excerpt, and relation-friendly blocks.

items.name: Evidence Entry
items.summary: Entry that presents support material, proof, or a captured artifact.
items.purpose: Provides a durable unit for source-backed reasoning, moderation support, inspection evidence, or public substantiation.
implementation_notes: Should foreground provenance, integrity, and support relations.

items.name: Classification Entry
items.summary: Entry used to label, categorize, or route a target or another entry.
items.purpose: Supports filing, triage, batch processing, and domain-specific controlled vocabulary actions.
implementation_notes: Often high-speed and often template-driven.

items.name: Social / Conversational Entry
items.summary: Entry used for discussion, reply, question, or collaborative exchange.
items.purpose: Lets XtraType support conversational interpretation and teamwork without collapsing into generic chat.
implementation_notes: Requires thread context and relation visibility.

items.name: Workflow Entry
items.summary: Entry whose primary role is to move work through a process.
items.purpose: Supports assignment, routing, moderation actions, approval states, and review outcomes.
implementation_notes: Workflow actions should read as actions, not hidden metadata.

items.name: Collection / Curation Entry
items.summary: Entry representing curation, grouping, ordering, or dossier assembly.
items.purpose: Lets users build notebooks, case files, boards, packets, and exhibit-like views.
implementation_notes: Collections are working surfaces, not just folders.

items.name: Media Entry
items.summary: Entry primarily centered on media objects or mixed-media capture.
items.purpose: Handles screenshots, photos, clips, recordings, transcripts, and AR or scan artifacts.
implementation_notes: May include generated blocks such as OCR or transcript content.

items.name: Assertion Entry
items.summary: Entry that stands as a claim, statement, proposition, or declared interpretation.
items.purpose: Provides a stable unit for claim review, argument mapping, corrections, and public challenge/support workflows.
implementation_notes: Should surface support, contradiction, confidence, and verification prominently.

items.name: Informational Intent
items.summary: Intent focused on recording or explaining something.
items.purpose: Optimizes the UI for note-taking, summarizing, and communicating context.
implementation_notes: Usually body-forward and support-optional.

items.name: Evaluative Intent
items.summary: Intent focused on judgment, assessment, or appraisal.
items.purpose: Supports inspections, reviews, ratings, and qualitative or comparative assessments.
implementation_notes: Often pairs with structured fields and evidence blocks.

items.name: Evidentiary Intent
items.summary: Intent focused on support, proof, or substantiation.
items.purpose: Prioritizes source attachment, provenance display, and support relations.
implementation_notes: Should open support surfaces by default.

items.name: Conversational Intent
items.summary: Intent focused on replying, asking, discussing, or debating.
items.purpose: Helps the UI emphasize thread context and social continuity.
implementation_notes: Needs thread and relation awareness.

items.name: Organizational Intent
items.summary: Intent focused on filing, grouping, saving, or arranging.
items.purpose: Supports collection work, memory workflows, batch classification, and curation.
implementation_notes: May minimize body text and foreground chips or collection controls.

items.name: Operational Intent
items.summary: Intent focused on moving work through action and process.
items.purpose: Supports queue routing, review movement, assignments, and moderation operations.
implementation_notes: Often appears alongside workflow entry classes.

items.name: Observational Intent
items.summary: Intent focused on reporting what was seen, measured, heard, or encountered.
items.purpose: Supports field note UX, route-aware capture, environment display, and scan/voice flows.
implementation_notes: Often pairs with spatial, temporal, and media blocks.

items.name: Referential Intent
items.summary: Intent focused on pointing, anchoring, or linking without extensive commentary.
items.purpose: Useful for lightweight saves, cross-links, and contextual pointers.
implementation_notes: Great for quick-save and overlay flows.

items.name: Corrective Intent
items.summary: Intent focused on amending, disputing, correcting, or superseding.
items.purpose: Powers error correction, dispute workflows, and public revision histories.
implementation_notes: Should foreground prior state and relationship semantics.

items.name: Personal / Mnemonic Intent
items.summary: Intent focused on saving something for later personal recall.
items.purpose: Supports personal memory and lightweight retention without forced publication or rigor.
implementation_notes: Often pairs with minimal friction and private visibility defaults.

items.name: Textual Modality
items.summary: Typed or pasted language content used as the main authored body.
items.purpose: Provides the most universal input mode across every surface.
implementation_notes: Text blocks should remain composable with all other modalities.

items.name: Media Modality
items.summary: Images, audio, video, clips, screenshots, and visual artifacts.
items.purpose: Enables richly contextual capture across browser, field, object, AR, and public workflows.
implementation_notes: Media should support metadata, preview, and derivative generated blocks.

items.name: Structured Modality
items.summary: Form-like, taxonomic, measured, matrix, or checklist content.
items.purpose: Supports inspections, templates, domain packs, and controlled vocabularies.
implementation_notes: Structure may be template-defined or ad hoc.

items.name: External Reference Modality
items.summary: External identifiers, URLs, bibliographic handles, imported references, and linked records.
items.purpose: Lets entries remain connected to outside systems and source materials.
implementation_notes: Should support preview and integrity-aware referencing.

items.name: Generated / Computed Modality
items.summary: Content derived by OCR, transcription, geocoding, extraction, or AI assistance.
items.purpose: Makes automation useful while keeping system-generated material inspectable and separable from user-authored content.
implementation_notes: Must always carry provenance and reversibility cues.

items.name: Spatial / Temporal Trace Modality
items.summary: Captured route, movement, path, time-series, or spatiotemporal trace content.
items.purpose: Supports field observation, roaming voice capture, AR anchoring, and reality-layer history.
implementation_notes: Should render cleanly in map, timeline, and detail views.

items.name: Capture Context
items.summary: Describes where the record began and what the initiating surface provided.
items.purpose: Lets the UI adapt to browser, mobile, workbench, import, scan, voice, overlay, or AR initiation.
implementation_notes: Capture context determines default blocks and disclosure level.

items.name: Domain Context
items.summary: Describes the operating domain or pack shaping terms, templates, and views.
items.purpose: Makes the same grammar feel native to research, fieldwork, moderation, classroom, public annotation, and other domains.
implementation_notes: Domain context changes feel, not semantic backbone.

items.name: Environmental Context
items.summary: Describes environmental and situational facts surrounding capture or use.
items.purpose: Preserves time, movement, weather-like situational notes, device state, and route facts when relevant.
implementation_notes: Environmental context is not always visible but should remain inspectable.

items.name: Privacy Context
items.summary: Describes the audience posture and publication expectations around a record.
items.purpose: Shapes warnings, publication affordances, visibility defaults, and overlay/public read behavior.
implementation_notes: Privacy context must remain visible whenever audience matters.

items.name: Collaboration Context
items.summary: Describes whether the current work is solo, synchronous team, asynchronous review, or public conversation.
items.purpose: Adapts threads, presence indicators, queue actions, and review affordances.
implementation_notes: Useful for live rooms, moderation queues, and classroom modes.

items.name: Presentation Context
items.summary: Describes whether the record is being authored, inspected, published, embedded, or overlaid.
items.purpose: Lets the frontend switch between editing, review, public read, ghost layer, and embeddable presentations cleanly.
implementation_notes: Presentation context should not mutate record meaning.

items.name: Source Type
items.summary: Class of origin material that can be cited, excerpted, or examined.
items.purpose: Standardizes how the frontend presents books, webpages, scans, datasets, clips, transcripts, and imported records.
implementation_notes: Source cards should remain reusable across entries.

items.name: Evidence Type
items.summary: Class of supporting artifact or proof unit.
items.purpose: Lets the UI distinguish images, excerpts, observations, measurements, testimonies, and system-derived evidence blocks.
implementation_notes: Evidence type should affect preview and trust cues.

items.name: Source Relation Type
items.summary: Relation that explains how a source connects to a claim, target, or entry.
items.purpose: Makes support, contradiction, attribution, translation, derivation, and citation semantics explicit.
implementation_notes: These relations must read in plain language.

items.name: Evidence Packet
items.summary: Curated bundle of sources and evidence blocks assembled for review or publication.
items.purpose: Supports moderation cases, dossiers, fact-check packets, field reports, and public proof bundles.
implementation_notes: Packets are collection-like but support-centric.

items.name: Evidence Chain
items.summary: Ordered chain showing how support flows from source material to extracted evidence to claim.
items.purpose: Provides reviewer-friendly trust navigation and public legibility for contested or rigorous records.
implementation_notes: Should have chain, compare, and graph-friendly renderings.

items.name: Entry-to-Target Relationship
items.summary: Relationship connecting an authored record to its subject.
items.purpose: Expresses attachment, anchoring, address, and contextual overlay semantics.
implementation_notes: Must survive movement across surfaces and views.

items.name: Entry-to-Entry Relationship
items.summary: Relationship connecting one record to another.
items.purpose: Supports replies, amendments, support, contradiction, duplicates, supersession, and thread continuity.
implementation_notes: Should work in list, thread, graph, and compare views.

items.name: Target-to-Target Relationship
items.summary: Relationship between two targets.
items.purpose: Supports aliasing, part-of, near, same-as, contained-in, related-topic, and route/place connections.
implementation_notes: Important for entity resolution and contextual exploration.

items.name: Source-to-Claim Relationship
items.summary: Relationship connecting a source or evidence unit to an assertion or claim.
items.purpose: Provides explicit support, contradiction, attribution, derivation, or translation semantics.
implementation_notes: This is a key relation in research and fact-check modes.

items.name: Collection-Membership Relationship
items.summary: Relationship connecting targets or entries to collections, boards, dossiers, queues, or notebooks.
items.purpose: Makes organization and curation first-class and queryable.
implementation_notes: Membership must carry ordering and role semantics when needed.

items.name: Supersession / Revision Relationship
items.summary: Relationship describing how one record replaces, revises, or retires another.
items.purpose: Supports corrections, public revision histories, moderation outcomes, and claim evolution.
implementation_notes: Revision lineage must be visible and human-readable.

items.name: Identity Mode
items.summary: Visible mode in which an actor appears in the interface.
items.purpose: Supports named, pseudonymous, anonymous, organizational, and system-generated participation.
implementation_notes: Display identity and verified identity may differ.

items.name: Authorship Attribute
items.summary: Attribution-related property describing how a record was authored or reviewed.
items.purpose: Keeps authored, co-authored, imported, generated, reviewed, and published contributions legible.
implementation_notes: Useful for trust surfaces and history views.

items.name: Reputation / Trust Attribute
items.summary: Attribute summarizing visible trust or standing around an actor or role.
items.purpose: Provides reader cues without replacing item-level trust semantics.
implementation_notes: Must never override record-level evidence and provenance.

items.name: Role Display Attribute
items.summary: Visible role label rendered beside an actor in the UI.
items.purpose: Helps users understand whether someone is contributing, reviewing, moderating, curating, or publishing.
implementation_notes: A display role is not the same thing as backend authorization.

items.name: Presence / Participation Attribute
items.summary: Attribute showing whether an actor is active in a live collaboration context.
items.purpose: Supports evidence rooms, live review, classroom sessions, and synchronous moderation.
implementation_notes: Presence is surface-specific and should degrade gracefully.

items.name: Draft State
items.summary: State indicating that a record exists but is not yet finalized for its current context.
items.purpose: Makes lightweight capture safe and recoverable across every surface.
implementation_notes: Every surface must support draft save.

items.name: Review State
items.summary: State indicating whether a record needs, is in, or has passed review.
items.purpose: Supports reviewer flows, moderation work, classroom checking, and trust-sensitive publication.
implementation_notes: Review state should be visible in queues and detail views.

items.name: Moderation State
items.summary: State indicating moderation posture or intervention.
items.purpose: Supports hidden, flagged, restricted, challenged, restored, or escalated content handling.
implementation_notes: Moderation should be explicit when it affects visibility or trust.

items.name: Resolution State
items.summary: State indicating whether an issue, claim, or workflow matter is open, resolved, or superseded.
items.purpose: Provides closure for cases, disputes, corrections, and action workflows.
implementation_notes: Particularly important in operational and corrective modes.

items.name: Publication State
items.summary: State indicating whether a record is private, staged, published, embedded, or archived for presentation.
items.purpose: Supports public read surfaces, overlays, dossiers, and shareable collections.
implementation_notes: Publication state should be clear but not conflated with visibility alone.

items.name: Assignment / Routing State
items.summary: State describing responsibility, queue placement, or route ownership.
items.purpose: Supports team review, moderation, classroom feedback, and operational routing.
implementation_notes: Useful in reviewer strips and queue views.

items.name: Visibility Class
items.summary: Audience visibility posture rendered by the frontend.
items.purpose: Makes it clear whether a record is private, shared, workspace-visible, public, or selectively exposed.
implementation_notes: Visibility should always be legible before publishing or sharing.

items.name: Permission Class
items.summary: Action capability class rendered to explain what can be done in the current interface.
items.purpose: Helps users understand edit, review, moderate, publish, export, and curate affordances.
implementation_notes: The frontend explains permissions even if enforcement is external.

items.name: Inheritance / Scope Rule
items.summary: Rule showing whether a setting is local, inherited, overridden, or collection-bound.
items.purpose: Prevents confusion when records live inside workspaces, collections, queues, or published bundles.
implementation_notes: Users should always know why a visibility or action rule is present.

items.name: Audience Class
items.summary: Named audience target used in public, team, classroom, and overlay publication flows.
items.purpose: Makes the act of addressing an audience explicit in the UI.
implementation_notes: Audience is related to but distinct from visibility.

items.name: Action Gating Class
items.summary: Frontend-visible class describing whether a meaningful action is allowed, restricted, or approval-bound.
items.purpose: Supports safe workflow handling around publish, verify, moderate, and promote actions.
implementation_notes: Action gating should appear inline, not only as an error after click.

items.name: Creation Provenance
items.summary: Record of how a target or entry came into existence.
items.purpose: Makes the frontend transparent about manual creation, import, scan, browser capture, voice capture, or generated derivation.
implementation_notes: Creation provenance should be concise in cards and deep in inspectors.

items.name: Technical Capture Metadata
items.summary: Technical details captured at creation time.
items.purpose: Preserves device, client, time, locator, quote, selection, scan, and route details that support trust and continuity.
implementation_notes: Metadata should be available without overwhelming the primary authoring flow.

items.name: Integrity Metadata
items.summary: Metadata supporting content integrity and chain awareness.
items.purpose: Enables hashes, snapshot references, edit lineage, and other trust-supporting signals.
implementation_notes: Integrity signals matter most in evidence and public trust flows.

items.name: Suggestion Provenance
items.summary: Provenance describing why a suggestion appeared and what input produced it.
items.purpose: Keeps automation explainable and reversible.
implementation_notes: Suggestions should say why they were suggested.

items.name: Transformation Provenance
items.summary: Record of meaningful transformations applied to content blocks.
items.purpose: Supports OCR, transcription, summarization, geocoding, extraction, redaction, and other computed steps.
implementation_notes: Transforms must never masquerade as raw capture.

items.name: System-Level Type
items.summary: Canonical type defined as part of the base XtraType grammar.
items.purpose: Provides stable semantics that survive across packs and surfaces.
implementation_notes: System-level types should change rarely and deliberately.

items.name: Domain-Level Type
items.summary: Type specialized for a domain pack while staying compatible with the core grammar.
items.purpose: Allows research, field, moderation, classroom, civic, and object-memory workflows to feel native.
implementation_notes: Domain-level types should inherit clearly from system-level types.

items.name: Vocabulary Class
items.summary: Controlled vocabulary or label system surfaced in templates and classification flows.
items.purpose: Keeps fast capture consistent without sacrificing extensibility.
implementation_notes: Should support suggestion, governance, and deprecation.

items.name: Template Class
items.summary: Reusable field and layout arrangement applied to authoring and review flows.
items.purpose: Turns the adaptive composer into a domain-native experience without breaking the core grammar.
implementation_notes: Templates may change disclosure, defaults, and blocks.

items.name: Domain Pack
items.summary: Bundle of templates, vocabulary, defaults, views, and terminology for a mode of work.
items.purpose: Lets the same product feel native across very different use cases.
implementation_notes: Packs specialize feel and structure, not semantics.

items.name: Proposal / Promotion Class
items.summary: Class representing a suggested new type, template, vocabulary, or pack element moving through controlled growth.
items.purpose: Enables expansion without chaos by making growth visible and governable from the frontend.
implementation_notes: Growth should move from local shape to shared concept through explicit steps.

items.name: Confidence Class
items.summary: Visible indicator of certainty or uncertainty around a record or claim.
items.purpose: Helps users separate tentative, observational, inferred, and strongly supported records.
implementation_notes: Confidence should be intelligible to casual readers.

items.name: Verification Class
items.summary: Visible verification posture indicating whether a record has been checked and by whom or how.
items.purpose: Supports public trust, review flows, and evidentiary rigor.
implementation_notes: Verification is not the same as truth or popularity.

items.name: Dispute Class
items.summary: Visible state indicating challenge, disagreement, or unresolved conflict.
items.purpose: Lets readers and reviewers understand contested records without erasing them.
implementation_notes: Dispute views should expose the conflict structure.

items.name: Quality Class
items.summary: Visible assessment of completeness, clarity, or readiness of a record.
items.purpose: Supports review queues, template hygiene, and public presentation quality cues.
implementation_notes: Quality should not hide underlying provenance or evidence gaps.

items.name: Sensitivity Class
items.summary: Indicator showing whether a record deserves extra caution in presentation or reuse.
items.purpose: Supports privacy-aware, safety-aware, and context-sensitive frontend behavior.
implementation_notes: Sensitivity affects presentation and warnings more than basic semantics.

items.name: Collection Type
items.summary: Named grouping surface that gathers targets or entries into usable working contexts.
items.purpose: Supports notebooks, boards, dossiers, queues, map layers, cases, and public bundles.
implementation_notes: Collections are active working surfaces.

items.name: Membership Relationship Type
items.summary: Relationship explaining how an item belongs to a collection.
items.purpose: Lets the frontend distinguish ordinary membership, ordered placement, pinned inclusion, or evidentiary bundling.
implementation_notes: Important for compare, queue, and dossier views.

items.name: Ordering Mode
items.summary: Mode describing how a collection is sequenced.
items.purpose: Supports manual ordering, time ordering, trust ordering, route ordering, evidentiary ordering, and more.
implementation_notes: Ordering is a first-class curation action.

items.name: Saved Lens
items.summary: Reusable filtered or perspective-driven view into a collection or target set.
items.purpose: Lets users move between memory, review, trust, public, spatial, or argument lenses quickly.
implementation_notes: Lenses are often more important than folders in a rich system.

items.name: Dossier / Packet Collection
items.summary: Collection optimized for assembled evidence, case material, or publishable packets.
items.purpose: Supports fact-check dossiers, moderation cases, classroom packets, and public proof bundles.
implementation_notes: Should support narrative and evidentiary ordering.

items.name: Layered Overlay Collection
items.summary: Collection optimized for overlaying many records on a page, place, route, or object.
items.purpose: Supports ghost layers, AR layers, map layers, and contextual public overlays.
implementation_notes: Layer visibility and density control are central.

items.name: Event Type
items.summary: Class for occurrences in the world or in the record universe that may themselves become targets.
items.purpose: Supports incidents, meetings, observations, changes, and public timelines.
implementation_notes: Event targets often combine spatial and temporal properties.

items.name: Activity Log Type
items.summary: Class for recorded actions taken in the system.
items.purpose: Provides inspectable trails for edits, reviews, promotions, publications, and moderation actions.
implementation_notes: Activity logs describe actions, not reality claims.

items.name: Session Event
items.summary: Event describing a meaningful boundary in a capture or collaboration session.
items.purpose: Supports live evidence rooms, roaming capture sessions, and recovery after interruptions.
implementation_notes: Helpful for continuity across complex surfaces.

items.name: Publication Event
items.summary: Event marking meaningful changes in public-facing presentation state.
items.purpose: Supports public revision histories, overlay updates, and published collection histories.
implementation_notes: Useful for public trust and embeddable history.

items.name: Target Axis
items.summary: UI axis answering what the record is about.
items.purpose: Determines target headers, address display, re-anchoring UI, and target-centric navigation.
implementation_notes: Always visible somewhere in the composer.

items.name: Intent Axis
items.summary: UI axis answering what the user is trying to do.
items.purpose: Drives defaults, visible fields, open drawers, and suggested actions.
implementation_notes: Intent is the main UI switchboard.

items.name: Support / Evidence Axis
items.summary: UI axis answering what support exists or is required.
items.purpose: Controls source trays, evidence prompts, trust cues, and claim substantiation flows.
implementation_notes: Particularly important in assertion and review modes.

items.name: Workflow / Privacy Axis
items.summary: UI axis answering what audience and process posture applies.
items.purpose: Shapes visibility cues, review actions, publication affordances, and warnings.
implementation_notes: Often shown as a visible strip rather than a hidden settings form.

items.name: Capture Surface Axis
items.summary: UI axis answering where the flow started and what affordances are native there.
items.purpose: Lets the system feel lightweight in browser/mobile while staying coherent in the workbench.
implementation_notes: Surface affects feel, not the core semantics.

items.name: Domain Pack Axis
items.summary: UI axis answering which pack or working mode shapes language and structure.
items.purpose: Allows one product to feel like many specialized tools without fragmenting the model.
implementation_notes: Domain pack switching should preserve the current record when possible.

items.name: Browser Extension Surface
items.summary: Capture-first surface for acting directly on webpages and digital resources.
items.purpose: Makes selection, quick note, citation, overlay, and save actions immediate in context.
implementation_notes: Must be fast, lightweight, and draft-safe.

items.name: Mobile Field Capture Surface
items.summary: Capture-first surface for geospatial, observational, camera, and movement-aware work.
items.purpose: Powers field notes, site inspection, roaming memory, and route-aware observation.
implementation_notes: Optimized for one-hand, interrupted, real-world use.

items.name: Manual Drafting Surface
items.summary: Focused authoring surface for deliberate creation and editing.
items.purpose: Provides a fuller editor without requiring the complexity of the full workbench.
implementation_notes: Useful when capture does not begin from a context.

items.name: Import and Share Surface
items.summary: Surface for turning shared or imported material into structured records.
items.purpose: Supports paste, share-sheet, upload, drag-drop, and imported external references.
implementation_notes: Should quickly extract a valid target and draft entry.

items.name: Target Workbench
items.summary: Deep exploration surface centered on one target and everything attached to it.
items.purpose: Lets users move between records, evidence, relationships, views, history, and public/read states.
implementation_notes: A target can support many lenses and many entry classes.

items.name: Collection Workbench
items.summary: Deep working surface centered on a collection, board, case, notebook, queue, or packet.
items.purpose: Supports curation, ordering, review, batching, comparison, and publishable assembly.
implementation_notes: Collections are a major navigation surface.

items.name: Reviewer and Moderation Surface
items.summary: Surface optimized for checking, routing, approving, challenging, or moderating records.
items.purpose: Supports queues, side-by-side comparison, trust inspection, and action trails.
implementation_notes: Should make state transitions explicit and legible.

items.name: Public Read Surface
items.summary: Presentation surface for shareable records, collections, overlays, and evidence bundles.
items.purpose: Lets XtraType function as a public contextual publishing layer without exposing authoring complexity.
implementation_notes: Public read must foreground trust, provenance, and revision context.

items.name: Object Memory Scan Surface
items.summary: Scan-first surface for attaching context to physical objects.
items.purpose: Enables object history, maintenance memory, field inspection, and situated notes.
implementation_notes: Should handle low-friction repeat visits to the same object target.

items.name: Voice-First Roaming Surface
items.summary: Capture surface optimized for spoken notes and movement.
items.purpose: Supports hands-busy or roaming workflows while preserving place, time, and observational context.
implementation_notes: Voice capture must remain editable and inspectable afterward.

items.name: AR Spatial Surface
items.summary: Surface for viewing and placing contextual records in spatial alignment with the environment.
items.purpose: Supports spatial overlays, place memory, guided inspection, and future reality-layer interactions.
implementation_notes: AR is a valid XtraType surface even if iterated later.

items.name: Ghost Layer / Overlay Surface
items.summary: Surface that overlays contextual records on pages, places, routes, or objects without entering a full workbench.
items.purpose: Makes XtraType feel like a living context layer over existing experiences.
implementation_notes: Overlay density and trust readability are critical.

items.name: Browser Capture Flow
items.summary: Flow beginning from a page, quote, or selection inside the browser surface.
items.purpose: Optimized for fast contextual capture with later escalation to deeper rigor.
implementation_notes: Should auto-build a draft with target context already attached.

items.name: Mobile Capture Flow
items.summary: Flow beginning from place, camera, route, or observation in the mobile surface.
items.purpose: Optimized for field use, interruption tolerance, and sensor- or place-aware context.
implementation_notes: Save must be possible with minimal typed input.

items.name: Manual Draft Flow
items.summary: Flow beginning from a deliberate new-entry action rather than a captured context.
items.purpose: Supports deeper creation, structured authoring, and thoughtful composition.
implementation_notes: Should offer target creation and target lookup as peers.

items.name: Import / Share Flow
items.summary: Flow beginning from pasted, shared, or uploaded content.
items.purpose: Quickly transforms imported material into targets, sources, drafts, or evidence blocks.
implementation_notes: Import suggestions should remain editable and attributable.

items.name: Voice-First Flow
items.summary: Flow beginning from spoken capture while moving or operating hands-free.
items.purpose: Lets users generate observational drafts without losing contextual fidelity.
implementation_notes: Must preserve transcript provenance and editability.

items.name: Object Scan Flow
items.summary: Flow beginning from scanning or tapping a real-world object anchor.
items.purpose: Supports repeatable object memory and scan-native inspection behavior.
implementation_notes: Should immediately resolve or create an object target.

items.name: AR Placement Flow
items.summary: Flow beginning from the user placing or opening a record in spatial alignment.
items.purpose: Supports future spatially anchored public or private contextual layers.
implementation_notes: AR placement must still end in an inspectable normal record.

items.name: Target Bar
items.summary: Always-visible region summarizing what the record is about.
items.purpose: Provides stable target context during authoring, review, and conversion.
implementation_notes: Users should never lose sight of the target.

items.name: Intent Rail
items.summary: Region for selecting or inspecting why the entry exists.
items.purpose: Turns intent into an accessible switchboard rather than a hidden field.
implementation_notes: Must remain editable in one click.

items.name: Main Authoring Pane
items.summary: Primary authoring region where entry content blocks live.
items.purpose: Hosts the body-first or structured-first editing experience depending on context.
implementation_notes: The main pane must support mixed modalities.

items.name: Support Drawer
items.summary: Expandable region for sources, citations, evidence, and support relations.
items.purpose: Keeps support first-class without overwhelming lightweight capture.
implementation_notes: Should open by default in evidentiary contexts.

items.name: Workflow / Privacy Drawer
items.summary: Expandable region for workflow state, audience, publication, and review actions.
items.purpose: Exposes governance without forcing every user into governance work immediately.
implementation_notes: Must show state clearly before any state-changing action.

items.name: Metadata / Provenance Drawer
items.summary: Expandable region for capture facts, transformations, and technical details.
items.purpose: Makes provenance inspectable without bloating the main authoring pane.
implementation_notes: Should clearly distinguish raw capture from computed content.

items.name: Relationship Tray
items.summary: Expandable region for reply, support, contradiction, supersession, and other relations.
items.purpose: Lets users express record graph structure without entering a dedicated graph editor.
implementation_notes: Needs human-language labels and sensible defaults.

items.name: Save / Status Rail
items.summary: Persistent region showing draft state, unsaved changes, and meaningful record state.
items.purpose: Provides confidence that lightweight capture is safe and deeper work is not lost.
implementation_notes: Must work consistently across all surfaces.

items.name: List View
items.summary: Dense sequential view of records or targets.
items.purpose: Supports quick scanning, filtering, batching, and memory retrieval.
implementation_notes: A universal fallback view.

items.name: Card / Detail View
items.summary: Focused view showing one item as a meaningful record card or detail page.
items.purpose: Provides the main reading unit across workbench and public read surfaces.
implementation_notes: Must expose target, intent, support, and governance clearly.

items.name: Board View
items.summary: Spatial or columnar arrangement for grouped workflow or curation states.
items.purpose: Supports moderation, triage, classroom review, and manual organization.
implementation_notes: Best when status or grouping is central.

items.name: Timeline View
items.summary: Chronological view of events, records, or revisions.
items.purpose: Supports event reconstruction, field logs, publication history, and memory review.
implementation_notes: Should handle both record time and target time gracefully.

items.name: Map View
items.summary: Spatial view of targets, observations, routes, and overlays.
items.purpose: Supports fieldwork, civic memory, object/place work, and spatial public layers.
implementation_notes: Needs density and layer controls.

items.name: Graph View
items.summary: Relational view emphasizing links among records, targets, sources, and claims.
items.purpose: Supports deeper exploration without making graph interaction mandatory everywhere.
implementation_notes: A specialized but important view.

items.name: Queue View
items.summary: Task- and workflow-oriented view optimized for review and processing.
items.purpose: Supports moderation, approvals, classroom feedback, and operational routing.
implementation_notes: Queue rows should surface trust and workflow state.

items.name: Compare View
items.summary: Side-by-side or diff-like view for examining multiple records together.
items.purpose: Supports review, correction, contradiction, duplicate handling, and evidence comparison.
implementation_notes: Particularly useful in review-heavy modes.

items.name: Gallery View
items.summary: Visual-heavy view for browsing media-rich records.
items.purpose: Supports field capture, object memory, public dossiers, and media-driven exploration.
implementation_notes: Should still preserve trust and provenance cues.

items.name: Dossier View
items.summary: Narrative or packet-like assembled view for curated record bundles.
items.purpose: Supports case files, evidence packets, publishable collections, and classroom bundles.
implementation_notes: Mixes narrative ordering with evidence structure.

items.name: Personal Memory Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for personal recall, contextual bookmarking, and lightweight retention.
items.purpose: Makes XtraType feel like a provenance-aware second memory layer.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: Research Notebook Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for source-backed reading, claims, citations, and evidence chains.
items.purpose: Turns the system into a structured research and synthesis environment.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: Field Observation Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for geospatial notes, measurements, media, and route-aware capture.
items.purpose: Makes the mobile and map-heavy system feel native to fieldwork.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: Fact-Check and Claim Review Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for assertions, support, contradiction, and verification workflows.
items.purpose: Supports claim review, public trust display, and corrective lineage.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: Public Annotation and Publishing Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for public read, shareable records, overlays, and publication state.
items.purpose: Lets XtraType function as a contextual publishing layer.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: Team Review and Moderation Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for queues, assignments, moderation states, and reviewer actions.
items.purpose: Supports operations-heavy collaborative workflows.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: Civic Place Memory Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for public or community place-based memory and layered local context.
items.purpose: Supports events, histories, observations, and public place layers.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: Classroom and Seminar Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for guided evidence work, collaborative reading, and feedback.
items.purpose: Turns XtraType into a structured learning and seminar environment.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: Schema Commons Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for templates, vocabularies, proposals, and controlled growth.
items.purpose: Makes type evolution and template stewardship an explicit working mode.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: Real-Time Evidence Room Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for synchronous collaboration around cases, claims, or target sets.
items.purpose: Supports live review, presence, and coordinated evidence handling.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: Object Memory Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for scan-based revisiting of physical items and their histories.
items.purpose: Supports maintenance, inspection, artifact memory, and place-object linkage.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: Ghost Layer Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for overlaying records on pages, places, and objects without leaving context.
items.purpose: Makes XtraType feel like a living contextual layer over existing environments.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: Voice-First Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for spoken capture, route-aware memory, and roaming observation.
items.purpose: Supports low-friction capture while preserving place, time, and trust context.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: AR Spatial Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for spatially anchored contextual records in augmented reality.
items.purpose: Supports future place-layer and object-layer experiences.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: Argument Operating System Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for claims, supports, contradictions, and interpretive structure.
items.purpose: Turns XtraType into a rigorous argument and reasoning environment when desired.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: Reality Layer Pack
items.summary: Domain pack optimized for large-scale layered context spanning pages, places, events, claims, and objects.
items.purpose: Represents the broadest sanctioned interpretation of XtraType as a contextual layer over the web and the world.
implementation_notes: Domain packs specialize feel, defaults, templates, and views without replacing the core grammar.

items.name: Intent Suggestion
items.summary: Suggestion proposing the likely purpose of the current record.
items.purpose: Speeds capture while preserving one-click override of the most important UI switchboard.
implementation_notes: Suggestions must say why they appeared.

items.name: Target Suggestion
items.summary: Suggestion proposing the best target identity or target refinement.
items.purpose: Helps resolve imported, scanned, selected, or ambiguous context into a usable target.
implementation_notes: Must remain editable and rejectable.

items.name: Support / Evidence Suggestion
items.summary: Suggestion proposing sources, excerpts, or evidence blocks that likely belong with the current record.
items.purpose: Makes evidentiary work faster without hiding source boundaries.
implementation_notes: Support suggestions should open the support drawer, not silently attach.

items.name: Template Suggestion
items.summary: Suggestion proposing a template, pack default, or field layout.
items.purpose: Lets the composer adapt intelligently without turning into a hidden rules engine.
implementation_notes: Template suggestions should be obvious and reversible.

items.name: Taxonomy Suggestion
items.summary: Suggestion proposing a new field, label, branch, or shared structure.
items.purpose: Supports controlled growth from local friction points.
implementation_notes: Local suggestion and global promotion must remain distinct.

items.name: Privacy / Audience Suggestion
items.summary: Suggestion proposing a likely audience posture or publication boundary.
items.purpose: Helps prevent accidental oversharing while speeding routine behavior.
implementation_notes: Privacy suggestions should always be conservative and explicit.

items.name: Workflow Routing Suggestion
items.summary: Suggestion proposing a next review, queue, or moderation action.
items.purpose: Supports operational efficiency in reviewer- and team-heavy modes.
implementation_notes: Must never silently perform high-stakes state changes.

items.name: Automation Boundary
items.summary: Explicit frontend rule describing what the system may suggest, prefill, stage, or auto-apply.
items.purpose: Prevents unwanted automation and preserves user trust in a highly adaptive interface.
implementation_notes: Automation must be explainable, reversible, and not quietly state-changing.

items.name: Contributor Role
items.summary: Primary role for people creating and editing records.
items.purpose: Anchors the everyday authoring experience across solo and collaborative modes.
implementation_notes: The baseline user-facing role.

items.name: Reviewer Role
items.summary: Role centered on checking records, support, and trust semantics.
items.purpose: Supports research, moderation, classroom, and publication workflows.
implementation_notes: Reviewer affordances should be visible but not universal.

items.name: Moderator Role
items.summary: Role centered on managing disputes, restrictions, and policy-sensitive states.
items.purpose: Supports public layers, team governance, and high-trust environments.
implementation_notes: Moderation visibility should remain explicit.

items.name: Publisher Role
items.summary: Role centered on public presentation, bundling, and publication transitions.
items.purpose: Supports public read, overlay publication, and public collection management.
implementation_notes: Useful where publication is distinct from authorship.

items.name: Workspace Steward Role
items.summary: Role centered on maintaining order inside a workspace or team context.
items.purpose: Supports shared defaults, template curation, and visibility hygiene.
implementation_notes: A visible UI role for growth without chaos.

items.name: Domain Curator Role
items.summary: Role centered on managing pack-level templates, vocabularies, and domain semantics.
items.purpose: Supports controlled specialization across many packs.
implementation_notes: Distinct from backend system admin.

items.name: System Steward Role
items.summary: Role centered on the shared canonical grammar and system-level structures.
items.purpose: Supports promotion, deprecation, and consistency across the whole frontend universe.
implementation_notes: Visible mainly in schema commons and governance-heavy surfaces.

items.name: User Defaults
items.summary: Persistent frontend preferences applied at the personal level.
items.purpose: Lets users tune capture behavior, disclosure, and routine defaults without changing shared semantics.
implementation_notes: User defaults are the most important personalization surface.

items.name: Capture-Context Settings
items.summary: Settings scoped to where a flow starts.
items.purpose: Lets browser, mobile, voice, scan, and import surfaces feel appropriate without one giant settings page.
implementation_notes: Capture-context settings should be discoverable in place.

items.name: Domain-Pack Settings
items.summary: Settings scoped to a pack or working mode.
items.purpose: Lets research, field, classroom, and public modes maintain different defaults and emphases.
implementation_notes: Domain settings tune feel without changing base semantics.

items.name: Template Settings
items.summary: Settings embedded in or attached to templates.
items.purpose: Let templates control disclosure, required blocks, and suggested behaviors.
implementation_notes: Template settings should remain visible during authoring.

items.name: Automation Settings
items.summary: Settings governing suggestion intensity and auto-apply boundaries.
items.purpose: Let users control proactivity without disabling useful assistance entirely.
implementation_notes: Automation settings must be granular and clearly explained.

items.name: Accessibility and Display Settings
items.summary: Settings governing readability, density, motion, contrast, and assistive behavior.
items.purpose: Ensures the expanded XtraType universe remains usable across devices and needs.
implementation_notes: Accessibility is a first-class frontend concern.

items.name: Public Record Presentation
items.summary: Public-facing presentation of a single record.
items.purpose: Turns an authored record into a durable, readable, trust-aware public page.
implementation_notes: Public view must expose provenance and revision cues.

items.name: Public Collection Presentation
items.summary: Public-facing presentation of a curated set of records or targets.
items.purpose: Supports published notebooks, dossiers, evidence bundles, and place layers.
implementation_notes: Collection narrative and trust structure both matter.

items.name: Overlay Presentation
items.summary: Contextual presentation layered on top of an existing page, place, or object.
items.purpose: Makes XtraType visible without forcing full navigation away from the underlying target.
implementation_notes: Overlay density and legibility must be carefully controlled.

items.name: Embeddable Card Presentation
items.summary: Compact public presentation intended for embedding elsewhere.
items.purpose: Lets records and collections travel while preserving a minimum trust and provenance frame.
implementation_notes: Must remain attributable and traceable back to canonical views.

items.name: Public Map / Timeline Presentation
items.summary: Public-facing spatial or chronological presentation of contextual layers.
items.purpose: Supports civic memory, route stories, event reconstructions, and reality-layer views.
implementation_notes: Needs strong filtering and context explanations.

items.name: Public Revision / History Presentation
items.summary: Presentation focused on visible change over time.
items.purpose: Supports trust by showing corrections, supersessions, publication events, and major review milestones.
implementation_notes: Crucial for public trust-sensitive content.
